title: narcissus tazetta subsp. lacticolor
intro: Narcissus tazetta subsp. lacticolor, also known as the white-flowered tazetta narcissus, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Amaryllidaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues and inflammation, due to the presence of alkaloids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Narcissus tazetta subsp. lacticolor include its ability to alleviate symptoms of colds and fever, as well as its potential to protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be prepared by steeping the flowers and bulbs in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
